title: The University of Texas at Austin - Full Episode | The College Tour
body: I came across this video when searching on "UT Austin campus tour." I was curious to know what the campus looks like today.  I graduated with master's degree from UT Austin in  1996 and lived there (in West Campus) until I moved across the country in 1999. I have never been back to Austin. I do not recognize some of the buildings in the video; they must have been constructed after my departure. In a way, my time there was a long time ago. In another way, it seems like yesterday. Going to UT Austin was a good experience for me, and I'm honored to have this school on my resume. GO HORNS!!!
author: @Geboren1971

title: Worst Universities in Texas #university #college #texas #fyp
body: I did a football camp at UNT. While I was there I saw the students using a lot of early enrollment recourses. The only thing that I personally think makes it a “bad college” are the dorm rooms. Dear goodness gracious. If you are arrested your prison cell will bear a striking similar resemblance to a UNT dorm. The beds are shaky and outdated held together by a rusty weld and the prayers that you don’t fall through the bed frame and the mattress will give you scoliosis. And the floors and corners are so dirty that eating off of a toilet seat is more hygienic. And the rooms are HAUNTED. I was there one year with my homie and our dorm was connected with another that was not being used. And I swear at night I could hear footsteps and creaking in that room, no one was in that room while we were there however. UNT for the love of EVERYTHING get some better dorms. Your dorm showers were A*S! THE TOILET PAPER GAVE ME THE WORST BUTTCRACK RASH EVER AND THE WHOLE TIME DURING SCRIMMAGES AT THE CAMP I WAS AT I HAD THE REEKING PAIN IN MY BUTTCRACK AND IN MY BACK FROM THE CRAPPY MATTRESSES, DO NOT LIVE IN THE DORMS FOR THE LOVE OF EVERYTHING
author: @D1NO_PRODUCTIONS

title: Psychiatry residents be like…. #residency day in the life of a psychiatry resident #nightshift
body: I’ve been considering psychiatry as my future residency. What kind of patients do you usually come across? Is it safe?
author: @coolscience622

title: Psychiatry residents be like…. #residency day in the life of a psychiatry resident #nightshift
body: I live in San Diego and the 3 major level 1 trauma centers here, do not have a Psych Dr. in house to call upon.  Frankly I feel that it's a shame, as mental health is so important! I would imagine that a patient that has the courage and takes the initiative to walk into the WE with Psych issues, must be extremely brave, and/or at their wits end. So I admire the job that you do!, it takes dtremghth and courage!
author: @9789-m

title: College Students Ask Me Tough Questions About God
body: Johnny, I spoke to you on the phone a long time ago. I'm here to apologize and tell you that I was very wrong in my judgments of you. These past days have been very trying times for me, and my faith was a little shaken. As soon as I went on YouTube, this video was at the top. I clicked on it, and when I heard you say, "Sin is trusting your heart above the word of God," the veil was lifted. I heard you say this before, but this time it spoke directly to my heart, and I received revelation of what it truly means. I saw that I was allowing myself to remain in depression because I was listening to my deceiving heart and mind. Thank you, my brother. I'm so sorry if I offended you. I see now that you are a man of God, and my judgments about you were very, very wrong. God bless you. I will be praying for you and your ministry.
author: @kaizenjeong

author: @RoVic9195

title: College Students Ask Me Tough Questions About God
body:
Not sure how you ended up on my page, but I saw the video of you preaching in Death Row, and it led me to this one. I clicked it because I'm in grad school now, but returned to God during my senior year of undergrad. A few months ago or so now, I had been saying to myself out loud that actions are triggered by feelings and feelings by thought. I told myself to stop sinning, I have to undo it one step at a time. I had to pause the video when I heard you say the same because I was in so much shock, and I just said, "Thank you God. Now I understand that was never something I learned but something you revealed to me in my times of obedience."Thank you for reaffirming that! I've never heard anyone else say something like it.

God bless you on your mission. I hope Jesus wraps his hands around you as you go forward with your purpose.
author: @le.02x

title: College Students Ask Me Tough Questions About God
body: I am dying but you are an inspiration for me to not to give up on God....carry it till the last breath
